title: Prasat Kuk
description: CISARK lists this temple as being a pink sandstone temple and located 300m northeast of Prasat Lbeuk Svay, however according to satellite imagery there doesn't appear to be any obvious sites of a temple site in that vicinity.

title: Prasat Lbeuk Svay
description: What remains of two brick temple towers where one can find the lower portion of the three walls still standing, a sandstone pedestal and a fragment of the sandstone lotus bud that would of adorned the top of the brick temple tower.

title: Prasat Banteay Preav
description: The site of a sizeable site that housed three brick temple towers, surrounded by a laterite enclosure with an east entrance pavilion. The towers no longer stands, and only a small portion of the walls can still be seen. The moated temple site is oriented

title: Tuol Prasat Ta Lop (Svay Chek)
description: The remains of a brick temple ruin where one can still see the lower sections of the walls still standing. The tower would of opened to the east, and is surrounded by a dried up moat.

title: Prasat Lbeuk Ampil
description: The site of a brick temple ruin surrounded by a possible laterite enclosure and a moat with an east entrance causeway that leads onto a filled rectangular man-made reservoir Trapeang Prasat Lbeuk Ampil 50m to the east.

title: Prasat Kok Prasat (Svay Chek)
description: Another three brick temple site, with fragments of brick, laterite and sandstone in situ, that is surrounded by a moat with an east entrance causeway that leads onto a smaller rectangular man-made reservoir Trapeang Kaun Kromom.

title: Prasat Sdau (Svay Chek)
description: The remains of a brick temple ruin, where one can still see the lower half of the walls still standing.

title: Prasat Ong Chak Khnear
description: The remains of a laterite ruin in the middle of the rice paddy fields.
